name: numpy-sugar
documentation:
# numpy-sugar

[![Documentation](https://readthedocs.org/projects/numpy-sugar/badge/?version=latest)](https://numpy-sugar.readthedocs.io/en/latest/?badge=latest)

Missing NumPy functionalities.

## Install

Enter

```bash
pip3 install numpy-sugar
```

from the command-line.

## Running the tests

Enter

```python
python3 -c "import numpy_sugar; numpy_sugar.test()"
```


## Authors

* [Danilo Horta](https://github.com/horta)

## License

This project is licensed under the [MIT License](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/limix/numpy-sugar/master/LICENSE.md).

variables.name: numpy_sugar.epsilon.super_tiny
documentation: Same as `numpy.finfo(float).eps`.

variables.name: numpy_sugar.epsilon.tiny
documentation: Same as `numpy.sqrt(numpy.finfo(float).eps)`.

variables.name: numpy_sugar.epsilon.small
documentation: Same as `numpy.sqrt(numpy.sqrt(numpy.finfo(float).eps))`.

functions.name: numpy_sugar._rankdata.nanrankdata
documentation:
Rank data for arrays contaning NaN values.

# Parameters

X

: Array of values.

axis

: Axis value. Defaults to `1`.

inplace

: Defaults to `False`.

# Returns

array_like

: Ranked array.

# Examples

```
.. doctest::

    >>> from numpy_sugar import nanrankdata
    >>> from numpy import arange
    >>>
    >>> X = arange(15).reshape((5, 3)).astype(float)
    >>> print(nanrankdata(X))
    [[1. 1. 1.]
     [2. 2. 2.]
     [3. 3. 3.]
     [4. 4. 4.]
     [5. 5. 5.]]
```

functions.name: numpy_sugar._testit.test
documentation:
Run tests to verify this package's integrity.

# Parameters

verbose

: `True` to show diagnostic. Defaults to `True`.

# Returns

int

: Exit code: `0` for success.

functions.name: numpy_sugar.linalg.diag.trace2
documentation:
Trace of $\mathrm A \mathrm B$.

Args:

: A (array_like): Left-hand side.
  B (array_like): Right-hand side.

Returns:

: float: Trace of $\mathrm A \mathrm B$.

functions.name: numpy_sugar.linalg.diag.sum2diag
documentation:
Add values `D` to the diagonal of matrix `A`.

Args:

: A (array_like): Left-hand side.
  D (array_like or float): Values to add.
  out ({class}`numpy.ndarray`, optional): copy result to.

Returns:

: {class}`numpy.ndarray`: Resulting matrix.

functions.name: numpy_sugar.linalg.det.plogdet
documentation:
Log of the pseudo-determinant.

It assumes that `K` is a positive semi-definite matrix.

Args:

: K (array_like): matrix.

Returns:

: float: log of the pseudo-determinant.

functions.name: numpy_sugar.linalg.cho.cho_solve
documentation:
Solve for Cholesky decomposition.

Solve the linear equations $\mathrm A \mathbf x = \mathbf b$,
given the Cholesky factorization of $\mathrm A$.

Args:

: L (array_like): Lower triangular matrix.
  b (array_like): Right-hand side.

Returns:
: {class}`numpy.ndarray`: The solution to the system

  : $\mathrm A \mathbf x = \mathbf b$.

# See Also

numpy.linalg.cholesky : Cholesky decomposition.
scipy.linalg.cho_solve : Solve linear equations given Cholesky

> factorization.

functions.name: numpy_sugar.linalg.dot.dotd
documentation:
Diagonal of $\mathrm A\mathrm B^\intercal$.

If `A` is $n\times p$ and `B` is $p\times n$, it is done in
$O(pn)$.

Args:

: A (array_like): Left matrix.
  B (array_like): Right matrix.
  out ({class}`numpy.ndarray`, optional): copy result to.

Returns:

: {class}`numpy.ndarray`: Resulting diagonal.

functions.name: numpy_sugar.linalg.dot.ddot
documentation:
Dot product of a matrix and a diagonal one.

Args:

: L (array_like): Left matrix.
  R (array_like): Right matrix.
  out ({class}`numpy.ndarray`, optional): copy result to.

Returns:

: {class}`numpy.ndarray`: Resulting matrix.

functions.name: numpy_sugar.linalg.dot.cdot
documentation:
Product of a Cholesky matrix with itself transposed.

Args:

: L (array_like): Cholesky matrix.
  out ({class}`numpy.ndarray`, optional): copy result to.

Returns:

: {class}`numpy.ndarray`: $\mathrm L\mathrm L^\intercal$.

functions.name: numpy_sugar.linalg.lu.lu_slogdet
documentation:
Natural logarithm of a LU decomposition.

Args:
    LU (tuple): LU decomposition.

Returns:
    tuple: sign and log-determinant.

functions.name: numpy_sugar.linalg.lu.lu_solve
documentation:
Solve for LU decomposition.

Solve the linear equations $\mathrm A \mathbf x = \mathbf b$,
given the LU factorization of $\mathrm A$.

Args:

: LU (array_like): LU decomposition.
  b (array_like): Right-hand side.

Returns:

: {class}`numpy.ndarray`: The solution to the system
  $\mathrm A \mathbf x = \mathbf b$.

# See Also

scipy.linalg.lu_factor : LU decomposition.
scipy.linalg.lu_solve : Solve linear equations given LU factorization.

functions.name: numpy_sugar.linalg.tri.stl
documentation:
Shortcut to `solve_triangular(A, b, lower=True, check_finite=False)`.

Solve linear systems $\mathrm A \mathbf x = \mathbf b$ when
$\mathrm A$ is a lower-triangular matrix.

Args:

: A (array_like): A lower-triangular matrix.
  b (array_like): Ordinate values.

Returns:

: {class}`numpy.ndarray`: Solution `x`.

# See Also

scipy.linalg.solve_triangular: Solve triangular linear equations.

functions.name: numpy_sugar.linalg.lstsq.lstsq
documentation:
Return the least-squares solution to a linear matrix equation.

Args:

: A (array_like): Coefficient matrix.
  b (array_like): Ordinate values.

Returns:

: {class}`numpy.ndarray`: Least-squares solution.

functions.name: numpy_sugar.linalg.solve.hsolve
documentation:
Solver for the linear equations of two variables and equations only.

It uses Householder reductions to solve `Ax = y` in a robust manner.

# Parameters

A

: Coefficient matrix.

y

: Ordinate values.

# Returns

{class}`numpy.ndarray` Solution `x`.

functions.name: numpy_sugar.linalg.solve.solve
documentation:
Solve for the linear equations $\mathrm A \mathbf x = \mathbf b$.

Args:

: A (array_like): Coefficient matrix.
  b (array_like): Ordinate values.

Returns:

: {class}`numpy.ndarray`: Solution `x`.

functions.name: numpy_sugar.linalg.solve.rsolve
documentation:
Robust solve for the linear equations.

Args:

: A (array_like): Coefficient matrix.
  b (array_like): Ordinate values.

Returns:

: {class}`numpy.ndarray`: Solution `x`.

functions.name: numpy_sugar.linalg.qs.economic_qs
documentation:
Economic eigen decomposition for symmetric matrices.

A symmetric matrix `K` can be decomposed in
$\mathrm Q_0 \mathrm S_0 \mathrm Q_0^\intercal + \mathrm Q_1\
\mathrm S_1 \mathrm Q_1^ \intercal$, where $\mathrm S_1$ is a zero
matrix with size determined by `K`'s rank deficiency.

Args:

: K (array_like): Symmetric matrix.
  epsilon (float): Eigen value threshold. Default is

  > `sqrt(finfo(float).eps)`.

Returns:

: tuple: `((Q0, Q1), S0)`.

functions.name: numpy_sugar.linalg.qs.economic_qs_linear
documentation:
Economic eigen decomposition for a symmetric matrix 𝙺=𝙶𝙶ᵀ.

Let us define

```
𝙺 = [𝚀₀  𝚀₁] [𝚂₀  𝟎] [𝚀₀ᵀ]
             [ 𝟎  𝟎] [𝚀₁ᵀ]
```

where the eigenvectors are the columns of [𝚀₀ 𝚀₁] and the positive
eigenvalues are the diagonal elements of 𝚂₀.

Args:

: G (array_like): Matrix.
  return_q1 (bool): Return 𝚀₁ matrix. Defaults to `True`.

Returns:

: tuple: ((𝚀₀, 𝚀₁), 𝚂₀).

functions.name: numpy_sugar.linalg.svd.economic_svd
documentation:
Economic Singular Value Decomposition.

Args:

: G (array_like): Matrix to be factorized.
  epsilon (float): Threshold on the square root of the eigen values.

  > Default is `sqrt(finfo(float).eps)`.

Returns:

: {class}`numpy.ndarray`: Unitary matrix.
  {class}`numpy.ndarray`: Singular values.
  {class}`numpy.ndarray`: Unitary matrix.

# See Also

numpy.linalg.svd : Cholesky decomposition.
scipy.linalg.svd : Cholesky decomposition.

functions.name: numpy_sugar.linalg._kron.kron_dot
documentation:
Kronecker product followed by dot product.

Let $\mathrm A$, $\mathrm B$, and $\mathrm C$ be matrices of
dimensions $p\times p$, $n\times d$, and $d\times p$.

It computes

$$
\text{unvec}((\mathrm A\otimes\mathrm B)\text{vec}(\mathrm C))
\in n\times p,
$$

which is equivalent to $\mathrm B\mathrm C\mathrm A^{\intercal}$.

# Parameters

A

: Matrix A.

B

: Matrix B.

C

: Matrix C.

out

: Copy result to. Defaults to `None`.

# Returns

{class}`numpy.ndarray`

: unvec((A ⊗ B) vec(C))

functions.name: numpy_sugar.linalg.property.check_symmetry
documentation:

functions.name: numpy_sugar._array.is_crescent
documentation:
Check if the array values are in non-decreasing order.

Args:

: arr (array_like): sequence of values.

Returns:

: bool: `True` for non-decreasing order.

functions.name: numpy_sugar._array.cartesian
documentation:
Cartesian indexing.

Returns a sequence of n-tuples indexing each element of a hypothetical
matrix of the given shape.

Args:

: shape (tuple): tuple of dimensions.

Returns:

: array_like: indices.

# Example

```
.. doctest::

    >>> from numpy_sugar import cartesian
    >>> print(cartesian((2, 3)))
    [[0 0]
     [0 1]
     [0 2]
     [1 0]
     [1 1]
     [1 2]]
```

Reference:

[1] <http://stackoverflow.com/a/27286794>

functions.name: numpy_sugar._array.unique
documentation:
Find the unique elements of an array.

It uses `dask.array.unique` if necessary.

Args:

: ar (array_like): Input array.

Returns:

: array_like: the sorted unique elements.
